pattern: Repeatedly peeling away the current outermost leaves shrinks the tree toward its centre; the one or two nodes that survive last are exactly the roots that minimise height.
hints: A root that minimises height must sit as close to the middle of the tree's longest path as possible.
Think about peeling leaves layer by layer, the way you would find the centre of a path.
Start from all degree-one nodes, remove them, and remove the new leaves they expose; whatever one or two nodes remain at the end are the answer.

edge_inputs.description: single node with no edges is its own centre
two nodes joined by one edge yield both as centres
star graph centres on the hub only
path of even length has two centres
straight chain of five nodes centres on the middle one
straight chain of four nodes has two middle centres

stress.description: random tree grown by attaching each new node to an existing one
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 20000))
    edges = []
    for v in range(1, n):
        u = rng.randrange(v)
        edges.append([u, v])
    rng.shuffle(edges)
    return [n, edges]
stress.note: attaching node v to a smaller-indexed node guarantees a single tree
